dev_plans.content:
Lightweight heuristic approach: compute per-race recall (TPR) directly from the validation set's ground-truth labels and predicted probabilities each epoch, then report the max-min gap. No external fairness libraries required — only numpy/pandas on top of the existing training loop.

What is measured: Among validation patients with true in-hospital death label=1, the True Positive Rate per race group, TPR_g = TP_g / (TP_g + FN_g). Equal-opportunity gap = max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g. A perfectly equal-opportunity model has gap 0; >0.10 indicates disproportionate missed deaths in some race group.

Where inserted: At the end of each epoch's validation pass, after `model.eval()` predictions on the held-out validation split. Race group is read from the existing `ETHNICITY` column in the MIMIC subject table, bucketed into 5 groups {White, Black, Hispanic, Asian, Other} using the same mapping the data loader already uses (so the probe does not re-introduce a new encoding).

Step-by-step:
1. After `val_preds = model(val_X)` and `val_probs = sigmoid(val_logits)`, also gather `val_ethnicity` (already in the dataframe) and `val_y_true`.
2. Binarize predictions at threshold 0.5 (or whatever threshold the trainer already uses for AUROC/AUPRC reporting — keep it identical so the gap is on the same operating point).
3. For each race group g present in the val split with at least N_min=30 positive-label patients (to avoid noise from tiny groups), compute TPR_g = sum((y_pred==1) & (y_true==1) & (g==g)) / sum((y_true==1) & (g==g)).
4. gap = max(TPR_g) - min(TPR_g) over qualifying groups.
5. Also compute overall val AUPRC (sklearn.metrics.average_precision_score) on the same val set; this is the anchor metric — the orchestrator rejects any 'fairness improvement' that drops AUPRC by >20% from the original checkpoint.
6. Log to the existing metrics CSV: columns `epoch, tpr_white, tpr_black, tpr_hispanic, tpr_asian, tpr_other, eopp_gap, val_auprc`.
7. Plot `eopp_gap` vs epoch as a line chart with two dashed reference lines (standard=0.05, acceptable=0.10).

Output: per-epoch CSV row with per-group TPR + gap + anchor AUPRC; one PNG plot of gap-vs-epoch with reference lines; an alert printed to stdout if gap exceeds 0.10 OR if val_auprc drops >20% from epoch 1's value.
dev_plans.metric: equal_opportunity_recall_gap = max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g on validation set among true-positive-label patients, computed per epoch (float in [0,1]).
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.05 — Hardt et al. 2016 'Equality of Opportunity' conventionally treats gap<0.05 as approximately fair; this is the bar a well-calibrated MIMIC in-hospital-mortality model with balanced group sampling should clear.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.10 — gaps above 0.10 are the Obermeyer et al. 2019 'dissecting racial bias' red line where the model is meaningfully missing deaths disproportionately; below 0.10 is OK-but-not-great.

dev_plans.content:
Statistically rigorous approach: bootstrap confidence intervals on per-group TPR and on the equal-opportunity gap, and report gap with a 95% CI lower bound (so PASS/FAIL is not driven by single-epoch sampling noise on small race groups).

What is measured: Same equal-opportunity gap (max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g) but with uncertainty quantified via stratified cluster bootstrap. Also computes a chi-squared test of homogeneity of TPR across groups to flag whether the gap is statistically distinguishable from zero at p<0.05.

Where inserted: Per-epoch validation pass, same location as the heuristic plan, but adds a post-validation bootstrap loop (1000 resamples, stratified by race group, sampling patients with replacement within each group). Adds ~2-5 seconds per epoch on typical MIMIC val sizes (~5-10k patients).

Step-by-step:
1. Gather val predictions, true labels, race group (same as heuristic plan).
2. Compute point-estimate TPR_g per group and gap.
3. Bootstrap: for b in 1..1000, resample val indices stratified by race group (with replacement), recompute TPR_g^(b) and gap^(b). Reject any resample where a group has 0 positive-label patients.
4. gap_ci_lower = 2.5th percentile of gap^(b); gap_ci_upper = 97.5th percentile.
5. Chi-squared homogeneity test: 2xG contingency table of (predicted_positive, predicted_negative) x race groups among y_true=1 patients; report p-value.
6. Anchor: val AUPRC with its own bootstrap 95% CI; revert the fix-loop if AUPRC CI lower bound drops >20% from epoch-1 point estimate.
7. Log CSV: `epoch, gap_point, gap_ci_lower, gap_ci_upper, chi2_pvalue, val_auprc, val_auprc_ci_lower`.
8. Plot gap-vs-epoch as a shaded ribbon (CI lower to CI upper) with dashed reference lines at 0.05 and 0.10.

Output: per-epoch CSV row with point estimate + 95% CI + chi-squared p-value; shaded-ribbon PNG plot; stdout alert if gap_ci_lower > 0.05 (statistically meaningful unfairness) or if chi2_pvalue < 0.01.
dev_plans.metric: equal_opportunity_recall_gap_ci_lower = 2.5th percentile of the 1000-resample stratified bootstrap distribution of (max_g TPR_g - min_g TPR_g) on the validation set, computed per epoch (float in [0,1]).
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.05 — the Hardt et al. equal-opportunity convention; using the CI lower bound (rather than point estimate) makes this a stricter, noise-robust bar that resists single-epoch flukes on small race subgroups.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.10 — the Obermeyer 2019 red line; using the CI lower bound means a model whose point-estimate gap is ~0.13 but whose CI lower bound is ~0.08 would still be flagged acceptable here, reflecting genuine uncertainty about the gap.

dev_plans.content:
Library-based approach: use the `fairlearn` library's MetricFrame to compute per-group recall (equal-opportunity) and the `selection_rate`/`true_positive_rate` disparity, plus fairlearn's built-in `difference` and `ratio` group-summary methods. This gives standardized, auditable fairness metrics with minimal custom code and integrates with fairlearn's downstream mitigation (reweighing/postprocessing) if the orchestrator later wants to actually reduce the gap rather than just measure it.

What is measured: per-race-group true_positive_rate (recall on y_true=1), via fairlearn.metrics.MetricFrame(metrics=sklearn.metrics.recall_score, y_true=val_y, y_pred=val_y_pred_binary, sensitive_features=val_race). Report `mf.difference(method='between_groups')` for TPR as the equal-opportunity gap, and `mf.ratio(method='between_groups')` as a complementary fairness ratio (min_g TPR / max_g TPR, 1.0=perfect). Also keep AUPRC as anchor via a separate MetricFrame on average_precision_score.

Where inserted: At the end of each validation pass. Requires adding `fairlearn>=0.9` to the project requirements. Computation is O(N_val * G) per epoch, negligible overhead.

Step-by-step:
1. Add `fairlearn` to requirements.txt.
2. In the validation epoch hook, gather val_y_true (binary), val_y_pred_binary (thresholded at the same operating point as AUPRC), and val_race (sensitive feature, pandas Series aligned to val indices).
3. Build MetricFrame: `from fairlearn.metrics import MetricFrame; mf_tpr = MetricFrame(metrics={'recall': recall_score}, y_true=val_y_true, y_pred=val_y_pred_binary, sensitive_features=val_race)`.
4. gap = mf_tpr.difference(method='between_groups')['recall'].  # max - min
5. tpr_ratio = mf_tpr.ratio(method='between_groups')['recall'].  # min / max
6. Anchor: `mf_auprc = MetricFrame(metrics={'auprc': average_precision_score}, y_true=val_y_true, y_pred=val_y_prob, sensitive_features=val_race)`; overall_val_auprc = mf_auprc.overall['auprc'].
7. Log per-group TPR via `mf_tpr.by_group['recall'].to_dict()`.
8. CSV row: `epoch, tpr_white, tpr_black, tpr_hispanic, tpr_asian, tpr_other, eopp_gap, tpr_ratio, val_auprc`.
9. Plot gap-vs-epoch (line) + tpr_ratio-vs-epoch (line, second y-axis or twin chart) with reference lines at gap=0.05 (standard) and gap=0.10 (acceptable), and ratio=0.90 (mirror of standard).
10. Revert safeguard: if overall val_auprc drops >20% from epoch-1 original, backend auto-reverts the checkpoint (anchor guard, already implemented in this repo).

Output: per-epoch CSV row with fairlearn-standard per-group metrics + gap + ratio + anchor AUPRC; two-line PNG plot (gap and ratio vs epoch) with dashed reference lines; stdout alert if gap > 0.10 or tpr_ratio < 0.90.
dev_plans.metric: equal_opportunity_recall_gap = fairlearn MetricFrame.difference(method='between_groups') on recall_score across race groups on the validation set, computed per epoch (float in [0,1]).
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.05 — the Hardt et al. equal-opportunity convention; aligns with fairlearn's own documentation examples where difference<0.05 on TPR is reported as 'approximately equal opportunity'.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.10 — corresponds to fairlearn's documented 'flag for review' threshold for binary classification group disparity; gaps above this are reported as actionable fairness violations in fairlearn's dashboards.
